message:
native: persistent Backend + per-shader microbench

NAPI surface adds:
  - createBackend()  → BigInt handle owning instance/adapter/device/queue
  - destroyBackend(h)
  - backendDispatch(h, name, ...) — same signature as dispatchByName
    but reuses the persistent backend (no per-call adapter init)
  - benchDispatch(h, name, ..., iters) → Float64Array of per-iter ms

Browser-side microbench (tests/conformance/bench-shaders.spec.ts +
.html) runs the same shaders via navigator.gpu, dumps medians to
fixtures/_browser-bench.json. Native bench reads + compares.

Result: native is 1.20× faster than browser per dispatch (geomean
across 15 shaders). Win came from dropping wgpuDevicePoll(wait=true)
between submit and mapAsync — the explicit blocking poll added
~1.27 ms regardless of shader. The mapAsync callback already implies
GPU completion; we just need to drive processEvents until it fires.

13/15 shaders win, 2 are within rounding (rms_norm at 1.35× browser,
matmul_int4_fp16_f16 at 0.99× — both small-input cases dominated by
encode latency). Conformance still byte-equal where applicable.
